_meta.note: Speculative-but-plausible PSP top-ups for 64 merchants with <4 PSPs. All additions are inference based on vertical, geography, and research context. Do NOT assert as fact to customers. Each merchant target: current_count + add_psps = 4. Stripe/PayPal/Apple Pay/Google Pay were intentionally varied with acquirers, BNPL, bank rails, and local APMs to avoid monoculture.

1password.reasoning: Cybersecurity SaaS B2C+B2B with global subs; PayPal is standard web fallback alongside Stripe cards and mobile IAP.

adobe.reasoning: Adobe operates its own billing stack but settles card volume via large-scale US acquirers; Chase Paymentech is a plausible anchor for Creative Cloud consumer volume.

amplitude.reasoning: Enterprise analytics SaaS; self-serve runs on Stripe cards, enterprise annual contracts use ACH and wire for large invoices.

asana.reasoning: Enterprise SaaS already on Zuora billing + Stripe cards + Avalara tax; ACH is the missing bank rail for annual invoicing.

bird.reasoning: Micromobility in 20+ EU/Middle East markets; Braintree covers cards+PayPal globally, Adyen likely present or needed for EU APMs (iDEAL, Bancontact, SOFORT).

box.reasoning: Enterprise cloud storage with self-serve tier; PayPal complements the card + bank-rail mix for SMB buyers.

buy-me-a-coffee.reasoning: Creator-tip platform, low-friction micropayments; Apple/Google Pay via Stripe are essential for one-click mobile web checkout.

calendly.reasoning: Has iOS IAP but Android IAP missing to complete the mobile stack alongside web via Stripe+PayPal.

care-com.reasoning: US caregiver marketplace with membership subs + caregiver payouts; needs ACH for payouts, PayPal/Apple Pay for consumer checkout on family side.

clickup.reasoning: Productivity SaaS, self-serve + enterprise. PayPal for self-serve, ACH for annual invoicing on enterprise tier.

deepl.reasoning: German SaaS with heavy EU user base; already has SEPA and bank transfer, PayPal is default consumer wallet in DACH.

descript.reasoning: Creator audio/video SaaS; currently Stripe-heavy. PayPal and Apple Pay complete the consumer wallet coverage.

digitalocean.reasoning: Developer cloud with global dev audience (US, EU, India, China). Alipay already covers China; Google Pay rounds out mobile wallets.

dropbox.reasoning: Global cloud storage with heavy EU subscriber base; SEPA is the natural recurring-billing rail for the EU Teams tier.

figma.reasoning: Design SaaS with enterprise tier post-Adobe. Annual invoicing requires bank rails; self-serve likely supports PayPal; SEPA for EU orgs.

flexport.reasoning: Freight forwarder with B2B invoicing in five to six figures; wire transfers dominate freight settlement; Plaid already enables ACH.

fox.reasoning: Media streaming (Fox Nation, Tubi) with CTV + mobile IAP already covered; Adyen is a plausible card acquirer for direct web checkout.

fundraise-up.reasoning: Nonprofit donation platform; ACH is standard for larger recurring donations alongside Stripe cards, PayPal, and Gemini crypto.

github.reasoning: Developer platform (Microsoft); enterprise seats settle via Azure/ACH; already has Stripe cards, PayPal, Azure marketplace rail.

hellofresh.reasoning: Meal-kit subscription, German origin, heavy DACH base; SEPA is dominant recurring rail for EU subs alongside Adyen cards + PayPal + Klarna.

hertz.reasoning: Car rental, global; Stripe covers web + terminal, legacy covers back-office. PayPal is common on European/US online booking flows.

hims-hers.reasoning: Telehealth subscription; Affirm is typical for higher-ticket treatment plans; PayPal for consumer trust in sensitive category.

hubspot.reasoning: CRM SaaS with own payments product built on Stripe; PayPal is the missing consumer wallet for self-serve seats.

instacart.reasoning: US grocery delivery; already has cards, BNPL, US acquirer; PayPal completes consumer-facing wallet options.

instantly.reasoning: Cold-email SaaS, SMB-focused, global user base. Stripe-only today; PayPal + Apple/Google Pay are the basic web-wallet expansion set.

intuit.reasoning: Intuit runs its own processing (QuickBooks Payments) but settles card volume via tier-one US acquirers; Chase is plausible anchor.

ixl-learning.reasoning: US K-12 ed-tech with family subs + school-district invoicing; PayPal for families, ACH for districts, iOS IAP for their app.

jane-app.reasoning: Canadian health-practitioner SaaS (deduped all three Stripe variants as Stripe+Stripe Express+Stripe Standard); Moneris is the dominant Canadian acquirer and complements Stripe for Interac support.

kajabi.reasoning: Creator course platform, higher-ticket digital products ($300-$2000); Klarna fits BNPL for course buyers; Apple Pay for mobile web.

legalzoom.reasoning: US legal services, ticket sizes $100-$500+ (LLC formations, wills); Affirm fits BNPL, ACH for business formation invoicing.

lovable.reasoning: AI app-builder, Swedish, global developer SaaS. Stripe-native stack; PayPal + Apple Pay round out consumer wallets.

lyft.reasoning: US ride-hailing; already multi-acquirer (Stripe, Braintree, First Data); PayPal completes the wallet stack alongside Apple/Google Pay via acquirers.

medely.reasoning: US healthcare labor marketplace with clinician payouts; Plaid is natural fit for bank account verification alongside existing ACH/Wire rails.

mentimeter.reasoning: Swedish audience-engagement SaaS with enterprise tier (Volvo, Unilever); SEPA for EU orgs, wire for enterprise annual contracts.

mews.reasoning: Czech hospitality PMS serving hotels in 85+ countries; Worldline is a dominant EU hospitality acquirer alongside Adyen and Stripe.

miro.reasoning: Collaborative whiteboard SaaS, Netherlands HQ, global enterprise; SEPA for EU, PayPal for self-serve consumers.

nexhealth.reasoning: US healthcare-patient-payments platform; CareCredit is the incumbent healthcare BNPL; ACH is common for dental/medical copays.

notion.reasoning: Global productivity SaaS; Stripe + mobile IAP + SEPA covers base; PayPal is the missing self-serve wallet for EU/LatAm consumers.

outdoorsy.reasoning: RV rental marketplace, US+EU, ticket size $150-$300/day with $500-$2500 deposits; BNPL (Affirm US, Klarna EU/AU) fits high-ticket leisure.

patreon.reasoning: Creator membership platform; web covered by Stripe+PayPal, iOS by Apple IAP; Android IAP is the obvious fourth.

perplexity-ai.reasoning: AI search SaaS with heavy global consumer subs; PayPal is the standard non-card checkout option for web subscription.

pipedrive.reasoning: Estonian CRM SaaS, global B2B; surprisingly no card processor listed — Stripe or Adyen would anchor card processing across EU + global.

pluralsight.reasoning: Tech-skills training SaaS, enterprise (Fortune 500) + individual; ACH + wire are needed for enterprise annual contracts.

poshmark.reasoning: US fashion resale marketplace; PayPal/Braintree for cards, Affirm for BNPL, Hyperwallet for payouts; Stripe as secondary acquirer fills out the stack.

quizlet.reasoning: Ed-tech consumer subs, student audience; already has mobile IAP on both platforms; PayPal is common for parents paying for Quizlet Plus.

raya.reasoning: Exclusive dating app, mobile-first; Android IAP missing, PayPal as web-wallet fallback for non-card members.

replit.reasoning: Developer cloud IDE, usage-based billing via Orb; Stripe for cards; PayPal for global devs, ACH for team/enterprise tier.

rippling.reasoning: HR/payroll platform; Plaid + ACH + wire is the standard payroll funding stack; Stripe handles card payments for SaaS subs.

squarespace.reasoning: Website builder + e-commerce platform with merchant checkout; Apple Pay + Afterpay are standard commerce-stack additions.

substack.reasoning: Newsletter/podcast subscriptions; has iOS IAP + Stripe web; Android IAP + PayPal complete the stack.

supabase.reasoning: Developer backend-as-a-service; deduped Stripe+Stripe Billing; PayPal for global devs, ACH for Team/Enterprise tiers.

superhuman.reasoning: Premium email client; high-ticket ($30/mo+); covers web + mobile IAP; PayPal adds consumer wallet option.

surepoint.reasoning: US legal-practice-management SaaS with embedded payments via LawPay + Payload; Stripe as secondary, ACH for high-value legal invoices.

taskrabbit.reasoning: Gig-economy marketplace; dual-acquirer for resilience; Apple Pay for mobile UX; ACH for Tasker payouts.

teachable.reasoning: Creator course platform like Kajabi; higher-ticket courses warrant Klarna BNPL; Apple Pay for mobile web.

thinkific.reasoning: Canadian course platform, similar to Teachable/Kajabi; Afterpay (Canadian/AU-friendly) over Klarna, Apple Pay for mobile.

turo.reasoning: US car-sharing marketplace, Stripe-native (payouts via Connect); high-ticket rentals benefit from BNPL; PayPal for international renters.

twilio.reasoning: Communications API, usage-based billing, heavy enterprise (US + global); ACH + wire are needed for large monthly invoices.

vercel.reasoning: Developer PaaS (Next.js company); deduped Stripe+Stripe Billing; PayPal for global devs, ACH for Enterprise tier.

wix-com.reasoning: Israeli website builder + commerce; global merchants; Stripe for US, MercadoPago for LatAm; Adyen is plausible EU anchor for Wix Payments merchants.

wolf-badger.reasoning: UK fashion marketplace for independent designers; cards + BNPL (Klarna + Clearpay) covered; PayPal is standard UK commerce wallet.

youtube.reasoning: Google-owned; own billing rail, plus mobile IAPs; PayPal is the standard external wallet offered at YouTube Premium/Music checkout.

zapier.reasoning: Automation SaaS with Team + Enterprise tiers; ACH is the typical US annual-billing rail alongside Stripe cards, PayPal, and wire.

zillow.reasoning: US real-estate platform; Plaid + ACH for rent payments / earnest-money deposits; PayPal for agent-facing SaaS tier.
